name: Sierra de Illón Loop
name_original: Vuelta a la Reserva Natural Foz de Benasa desde Navascués por la Foz de Burgui
description: This difficult 30.5-mile road cycling route from Navascués through Foz de Burgui offers challenging climbs and stunning gorge views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Navascués traverse the Pyrenean foothills in the Navarre region of Northern Spain, offering diverse landscapes and challenging terrain. The area is characterized by dramatic canyons like Foz de Arbayun and Foz de Burgui, alongside mountains, forests, and valleys. Cyclists can expect well-maintained, low-traffic roads that provide access to significant elevation gains and scenic vistas. Best road cycling routes around Navascués The most popular road cycling route is Tour through the Salazar Valley from Navascués to the port of Laza, a 50.5 miles (81.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features demanding climbs through the Salazar Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Return to the Natural Reserve Foz de Benasa from Navascués through the Foz de Burgui, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Foz de Burgui, a natural reserve known for its rugged beauty. Local road cyclists also love the Pierre Saint-Martin Pass – Col de la Pierre Saint-Martin loop from Salvatierra de Esca, a 65.9 miles (106.0 km) trail leading through the Pyrenean foothills, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Road cycling around Navascués is defined by challenging climbs, dramatic canyons, and well-maintained roads. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation gain. The routes in Navascués are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Navascués's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Navascués?
A: Road cycling around Navascués primarily involves challenging terrain within the Pyrenean foothills. You'll encounter a mix of mountains, forests, and valleys, with routes featuring significant elevation gain. The region is known for its well-maintained, low-traffic minor roads, providing a scenic and immersive cycling experience.

Q: Are there many challenging road cycling routes in the Navascués area?
A: Yes, Navascués is well-suited for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. The area features numerous 'hard road rides' with substantial elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985147603/tour-through-the-salazar-valley-from-navascues-to-the-port-of-laza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour through the Salazar Valley from Navascués to the port of Laza&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 81.3 km route with over 1,100 meters of ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba Village – Laza Pass (1,129 m) loop from Gallués / Galoze&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 160 km with nearly 2,700 meters of climbing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes near Navascués?
A: The routes around Navascués offer stunning natural beauty. You can cycle past impressive canyons like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayun&lt;/a&gt;, a significant natural wonder carved by the Salazar River, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Burgui&lt;/a&gt;, a natural reserve known for its vulture colonies. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5779753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leyre Monastery&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2001608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes provide breathtaking views of the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes around Navascués?
A: The road cycling routes around Navascués are primarily defined by challenging climbs and significant elevation gain, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists. Currently, there are no routes classified as 'easy' on komoot for this region, with 9 moderate and 10 difficult routes available. If you're looking for less strenuous options, consider exploring other cycling disciplines or areas with flatter terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about cycling in Navascués?
A: The road cycling routes in Navascués are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the dramatic canyons like Foz de Arbayun and Foz de Burgui, and the well-maintained, low-traffic roads that allow for an immersive experience in the stunning Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Navascués?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pierre Saint-Martin Pass – Col de la Pierre Saint-Martin loop from Salvatierra de Esca&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 106 km route through the Pyrenean foothills. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba Village – Urzainqui Village loop from Salvatierra de Esca&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 50.8 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Navascués?
A: Given its location in the Pyrenean foothills, the best time for road cycling in Navascués is typically during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. These seasons offer more favorable weather conditions, with less chance of snow or extreme cold at higher elevations, allowing for comfortable exploration of the challenging terrain and scenic routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options in the Navascués area?
A: Yes, Navascués offers routes suitable for long-distance road cycling. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isaba Village – Laza Pass (1,129 m) loop from Gallués / Galoze&lt;/a&gt; is a significant ride covering over 160 km with substantial elevation gain, perfect for those looking for an extended challenge.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife while road cycling in Navascués?
A: The natural reserves and canyons around Navascués, such as the Foz de Burgui, are known for their rich wildlife. The Foz de Burgui is home to one of Europe's largest colonies of vultures. While road cycling, you'll be immersed in natural landscapes, increasing your chances of observing local fauna, especially in quieter sections of the routes.

Q: Where can I find more information about cycling in the Navarre region?
A: For additional information about cycling opportunities in the broader Navarre region, you can visit the official tourism website: &lt;a href="https://www.visitnavarra.es/en/navarra-en-bici-carretera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitnavarra.es&lt;/a&gt;. This resource provides context on the region's appeal for cyclists, including its diverse landscapes and well-maintained roads.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Navascués?
A: Many routes in the Navascués area offer spectacular viewpoints, especially those traversing the canyons and Pyrenean foothills. The Foz de Arbayun, for example, has viewpoints situated at 630 meters above sea level, providing breathtaking panoramic views of the canyon. Cycling routes in the vicinity allow you to access these scenic spots.
tips: Beautiful road that runs through the spectacular Foz de Burgui Nature Reserve
Burgi (or Burgui) is a beautiful and picturesque town located on the banks of the Ezka River. It is the ideal place to start your journey through this area and reach the beautiful Foz de Burgui.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'difficult' and features a significant elevation gain of 950 meters over approximately 49 kilometers. While road surfaces are generally well-paved, expect sustained climbs and potentially steep sections, with some areas indicating gradients of up to 10%.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires a 'very good fitness' level due to its substantial elevation gain and challenging climbs. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and captivating landscapes. Key features include the magnificent cliffs of Views of the Foz de Burgui, carved by the Esca River, and the natural reserve of Foz de Benasa. The route also passes through Burgui, a settlement with historical elements.

faqs.question: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, within the Foz de Burgui, you can admire the old medieval bridge of Burgui. There's also a 3-kilometer stretch known as the 'Promenade of Crafts' with reproductions illustrating traditional trades like Almadieros (rafters) and charcoal burners, offering a unique cultural dimension to your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot along the route?
faqs.answer: The Foz de Burgui is renowned for its rich birdlife. You'll likely see numerous rock-dwelling birds, including one of Europe's largest vulture colonies, particularly around the cliffs of Borreguil and Virgen de la Peña.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Navascués and returns to the Natural Reserve Foz de Benasa. The Foz de Benasa area near Navascués features a recreational area with picnic spots, which could serve as a convenient starting and ending point with parking facilities.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in the Foz de Benasa Natural Reserve?
faqs.answer: The available information does not indicate that special permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling through the Foz de Benasa Natural Reserve or the Foz de Burgui. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations before your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature of the route and the mountainous terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring adverse weather conditions to the Pyrenean foothills.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the settlement of Burgui, where you may find options for cafes or restaurants. Navascués, the starting point, is also likely to have amenities. The Foz de Benasa recreational area offers picnic spots for a stop after your ride.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, specific rules for dogs on bikes or within the natural reserves (Foz de Benasa and Foz de Burgui) are not detailed. It's best to check local regulations regarding pets in these protected areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse, Foz de Burgui, and Cañada de los Roncaleses, among others.

waypoints.name: Views of the Foz de Burgui
